text: Motivated by the recent synthesis of $X\text{B}_3\text{C}_3$ ($X = \text{Sr}, \text{La}$) in bipartite sodalite clathrate structures, we employ density functional theory (DFT) to explore this chemical family, including derivatives containing two distinct metal species. A DFT-chemical pressure analysis of systems where $X = \text{Mg}, \text{Ca}, \text{Sr}, \text{Ba}$ demonstrates that the size of the metal cation is a critical factor for ambient-pressure stability, as the guest must be properly sized to support the B–C framework. High-throughput screening of 105 binary-guest $XY\text{B}_6\text{C}_6$ compounds ($Pm\bar{3}$ symmetry) identified 22 dynamically stable candidates at 1 atm, significantly expanding the pool of potentially realizable phases by 19 (comprising 18 metals and 1 insulator). Our results show that the electronic density of states at the Fermi level and the superconducting transition temperature ($T_c$) can be effectively tuned by adjusting the average oxidation state of the metal atoms, with $T_c$ peaking at an average valence of +1.5. Among all studied $Pm\bar{3}n$ $X\text{B}_3\text{C}_3$ and $Pm\bar{3}$ $XY\text{B}_6\text{C}_6$ phases, $\text{KPbB}_6\text{C}_6$ stands out with a predicted Eliashberg $T_c$ of 88 K at ambient pressure. Further analysis suggests that $\text{KPbB}_6\text{C}_6$ is a viable target for high-pressure, high-temperature synthesis followed by quenching to ambient conditions.
